texto: And the stars: the sky gets crowded at night, and it is a bit like watching a clock, seeing the constellations slide across the sky. It’s comforting to know that they’ll show up, however bad the day has been, however crook things get. That used to help in France. It put things into perspective—the stars had been around since before there were people. They just kept shining, no matter what was going on. I think of the light here like that, like a splinter of a star that’s fallen to earth: it just shines, no matter what is happening. Summer, winter, storm, fine weather. People can rely on it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a M.L. Stedman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de M.L. Stedman sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “If the war had taught her anything, it was to take nothing for granted: that it was…

frase_original: If the war had taught her anything, it was to take nothing for granted: that it wasn’t safe to put off what mattered. Life could snatch away the things you treasured, and there was no getting them back.
